nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: Golden-capped parakeet
nodes.summary: Golden-capped parakeet（Golden-capped parakeet，学名 Aratinga auricapillus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、森林砍伐、小种群风险。

nodes.name: 小种群风险
nodes.summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: It is threatened by habitat loss

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Though observed to be in decline due to deforestation based on data recorded in affected areas, golden-capped parakeets are more commonly found outside primary forests and have a distribution of population throughout Guyana, Brazil, and Paraguay. During a 2018 published survey of local bird species in Minas Gerais, Brazil, golden-capped parakeets received an Occurrence Frequency of "Frequent" throughout the region, and other surveys reporting sightings in the Chapada Diamantina National Park and the Serra de Ouricana have revealed that the Aratinga auricapillus' small population relatively manages to maintain its numbers.{{Cite journal|last1=Gonzaga|first1=L

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Though observed to be in decline due to deforestation based on data recorded in affected areas, golden-capped parakeets are more commonly found outside primary forests and have a distribution of population throughout Guyana, Brazil, and Paraguay. During a 2018 published survey of local bird species in Minas Gerais, Brazil, golden-capped parakeets received an Occurrence Frequency of "Frequent" throughout the region, and other surveys reporting sightings in the Chapada Diamantina National Park and the Serra de Ouricana have revealed that the Aratinga auricapillus' small population relatively manages to maintain its numbers.{{Cite journal|last1=Gonzaga|first1=L
